query: As of early July 2026, what percentage of Russia's national oil-refining capacity is offline/disabled due to Ukrainian drone strikes?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the findings:

## Answer: ~43% (official Ukrainian claim) / ~one-third (independent consensus)

As of early July 2026, the **most-cited headline figure is 42.74% ("nearly 43%")** of Russia's total designed oil-refining capacity disabled — announced by **Ukraine's General Staff on July 4, 2026** (cumulative since August 2025).

However, this is a Ukrainian-government figure using a "design throughput struck" methodology, and it sits at the **high end of a range of estimates**:

- **~50%** — Ukrainian analyst Mykhailo Honchar (June 30)
- **~43%** — Ukraine General Staff (July 4)
- **~33–40%** — IEA and Reuters-cited industry sources (independent consensus)
- **~one-third** — Energy Intelligence (2.14 million b/d) and Macro-Advisory's Chris Weafer
- **~28%** — CEPA (capacity loss vs. prior years after mid-June strikes)
- **~20–25%** — Razumkov Centre's Volodymyr Omelchenko (most conservative)

**Key caveat**: The precise number hinges on methodology. "Capacity struck/damaged" figures (higher) overstate the actual reduction in refining, because Russia repairs facilities and reroutes crude. The most defensible **independent "currently offline" estimate is roughly one-third (~33–40%)**.

Supporting evidence of severity: refining runs fell below 4 million b/d in early June (lowest in 21 years), gasoline output dropped ~25% YoY, 8 of Russia's 10 largest refineries were hit, a nationwide fuel crisis triggered rationing in over half of Russia's regions, and cumulative industry losses reached ~$13.5 billion.

sources.title: Ukrainian Defense Forces Disable 43% of Russia's Total Oil Refining Capacity
sources.snippet: The General Staff of the Armed Forces of Ukraine announced that ongoing strikes have disabled 42.7% of Russia's total oil refining capacity, inflicting an estimated $13.5 billion in industry losses since August 2025.

sources.title: General Staff says Ukraine knocked out 42% of Russia's refining and cost industry $13.5 billion since August 2025
sources.snippet: Ukrainian strikes have idled 42.74% of Russia's oil refining. The figure sits between Honchar's 50% and IEA/Reuters' 33–40%. OilX: refinery runs averaged 4.58 million b/d in May, lowest since 2009.

sources.title: Ukraine burned half of Russia's oil refining, Ukrainian analyst says
sources.snippet: Half of Russia's primary oil refining capacity is now offline, according to Mykhailo Honchar; IEA and Reuters put offline capacity at roughly 33-40% through June.

sources.title: Russian Refining: Drones Knock Out One-Third of Primary Capacity
sources.snippet: Nearly one-third, or 2.14 million b/d, of Russia's crude oil refining capacity is currently off line because of Ukrainian drone attacks. Refining runs in the first week of June plunged below 4 million b/d, lowest in 21 years.

sources.title: Running on Empty: Russia's Fuel Crisis
sources.snippet: After the Kapotnya and TANECO strikes in mid-June, the loss of capacity may have reached 28% against prior years. Eight of Russia's 10 largest refineries have been affected.

sources.title: Ukrainian drone attacks on oil refineries plunge Russia into a summer fuel crisis
sources.snippet: About a third of Russia's oil refining capacity is offline, said Chris Weafer, CEO of Macro-Advisory Ltd.

sources.title: Russia lost 20–25% of its oil refining capacities
sources.snippet: Volodymyr Omelchenko: Russia lost 20-25% of all its current oil refining capacities; over 60 million tonnes/year no longer operational.

sources.title: Gasoline production in Russia falls by 25% after Ukraine steps up drone attacks
sources.snippet: Reuters: gasoline output fell 25% YoY in first 20 days of June; daily output fell from ~120,000 t/day in March to ~85,000 t/day after Moscow Oil Refinery and TANECO halts.

sources.title: Ukraine Strikes Russia's Largest Omsk Oil Refinery for the First Time
sources.snippet: Omsk refinery processes more than 22 million tonnes of crude annually and accounts for roughly 10% of the country's total refining capacity; struck July 6, 2026.

sources.title: Have Ukrainian Drones Really Knocked Out 38% of Russia's Oil Refining Capacity?
sources.snippet: Sergey Vakulenko: Russia can refine 327 million tons of oil every year; headline 'capacity hit' figures overstate actual refining reduction.

sources.title: 2025–2026 Russian fuel crisis
sources.snippet: Starting August 2025, Ukrainian drone attacks caused significant decrease in refined products; crisis escalated mid-2026, Crimea declared state of emergency in June.
